home.title: The pulse of the crypto market, in your wallet.
home.subtitle: A non-custodial demo exchange: live prices, on-chain staking on Sepolia, and paper-trading bots. We never hold your funds or ask for your seed.

converter.title: Converter
converter.subtitle: Convert any crypto to US dollars and Argentine pesos, using the dollar rate you choose.
converter.invalidAmount: Enter a number greater than zero.
converter.disclaimer: Market prices (CoinGecko/Binance) and dollar rates (CriptoYa) with cache delay. Reference only — not an exchange offer.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Your holdings in the connected wallet (Sepolia), valued in dollars and pesos with live market prices.

connect.title: Connect your wallet to see your portfolio
connect.desc: We read your on-chain balances on Sepolia — non-custodial, we never ask for your seed or move funds.

portfolio.wrongNetwork: Switch your wallet to the Sepolia network to see your balances.
portfolio.totalHint: Only ETH is valued (it has a market price). The PULSO token is testnet and has no quote.
portfolio.disclaimer: On-chain Sepolia data + market prices (CoinGecko/CriptoYa) with cache delay. Reference only.

staking.title: Staking
staking.subtitle: Stake test PULSO on Sepolia and earn rewards in the same token. Non-custodial: every action is a transaction you sign, PULSO never touches your funds.

connect.title: Connect your wallet
connect.desc: You need a wallet connected to Sepolia Testnet to claim PULSO from the faucet and stake.

stake.desc: Lock PULSO to start earning rewards.
stake.insufficientBalance: You don't have enough PULSO available.

tx.reverted: The transaction was reverted on-chain. Check the conditions (cooldown, balance) and try again.
tx.unexpectedError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

wrongNetwork.message: Your wallet is on {{network}} — PULSO runs on Sepolia Testnet.
wrongNetwork.detail: Switch networks to claim from the faucet, stake and collect rewards. No action is enabled on the wrong network.

trends.subtitle: Market sentiment, trending coins, 24h gainers/losers and BTC dominance.
trends.gaugeAriaEmpty: Fear and greed index with no data
trends.fearGreedDesc: Aggregate crypto market sentiment, via alternative.me.

bots.title: Bots
bots.subtitle: Paper trading engine: DCA, Grid and SMA Crossover on real Binance prices.
bots.banner: PAPER TRADING — Funds and operations are fully simulated. No PULSO bot connects to a real exchange or executes real orders.
bots.emptyTitle: You haven't created any bots yet
bots.emptyDescription: Get started with DCA, Grid or SMA — all with simulated funds.
bots.noEquityHistory: There's no equity history for this bot yet.

DCA.label: DCA
DCA.desc: Buys a fixed USD amount at a set interval, regardless of price.

GRID.label: Grid
GRID.desc: Fixed buy/sell levels above and below the current price.

SMA.label: SMA Crossover
SMA.desc: Moving average crossover: buys on the bullish cross, sells on the bearish one.

grid.note: Levels are calculated from the pair's current price at the moment the bot is created.

sma.invalid: The short SMA must be less than the long SMA.

detail.noTradesDesc: The engine hasn't executed any simulated order for this bot yet.
detail.confirmDelete: Delete this bot and its history?

security.title: Security
security.intro: PULSO is non-custodial: we never have access to your funds or your keys. You sign every on-chain action yourself, from your own wallet. This page explains in detail what that means in practice.

custodialModel.heading: How the non-custodial model works
custodialModel.desc: Your wallet (MetaMask, Rainbow, etc.) always keeps your keys on your own device. PULSO never sees them, never asks for them, and could never move them even if it wanted to.
custodialModel.p1: When you connect your wallet, the app only reads your public address and balances — information anyone can see on a block explorer. No operation (faucet, stake, claim, etc.) runs on its own: each one opens a popup in your wallet where you see exactly which contract, which function and which amount you're about to sign, and you decide whether to confirm or reject.
custodialModel.p2: If PULSO disappeared tomorrow, your staked PULSO and your rewards would still be yours: they live in the contracts on Sepolia, not on one of our servers. PULSO's backend never holds a private key or the ability to move a single token.

signedActions.heading: What you sign, action by action
signedActions.desc: Everything that happens in the Staking module boils down to these five transactions. Nothing else moves your funds.

faucet.what: You request 100 test PULSO. It only moves testnet tokens to your wallet — never the other way around.

approve.what: You authorize the Staking contract to move a certain amount of PULSO on your behalf. Nothing moves yet: it only grants permission.

stake.what: You transfer PULSO from your wallet to the Staking contract. It's recorded under your name and you can withdraw it any time with Unstake.

claim.what: You withdraw to your wallet the rewards you've already earned. It doesn't touch your staked principal.

unstake.what: Returns your staked PULSO (and pending rewards, in the case of Exit) to your wallet. Never blocked by a contract pause.

neverAsks.heading: PULSO will never ask you for:
neverAsks.item1: Your seed phrase or recovery words.
neverAsks.item2: Your private key, in any format or through any channel.
neverAsks.item3: To "connect your wallet here" outside the official PULSO app.
neverAsks.item4: To transfer funds to a "support" or "verification" address.
neverAsks.item5: Passwords, 2FA codes or access to your centralized exchange.
neverAsks.footer: If someone asks you for any of this claiming to be "from PULSO" (via Telegram, Discord, DM or any other channel), it's a scam attempt — ignore it and don't reply.

verifyContracts.heading: How to verify the contracts yourself
verifyContracts.desc: Don't trust, verify: both PULSO contracts are publicly verified on Etherscan. Anyone can read the exact source code running on-chain, without relying on what this page says.
verifyContracts.cardDesc: Verified addresses — the source code matches byte-for-byte with what's deployed.
verifyContracts.step1: Open the "View on Etherscan" link for either contract above.
verifyContracts.step2a: Look for the green checkmark icon next to "Contract" — it means the source code is verified (tab
verifyContracts.step3: Read the code directly there, or compare it with the project's public repository (contracts/src folder).
verifyContracts.step4c: tab you can query your staked balance, pending rewards, etc. directly from Etherscan, without going through the PULSO app.

disclaimers.heading: Disclaimers
disclaimers.desc: PULSO is a demonstration project. Nothing below is negotiable, and it applies no matter what part of the app you're in.
disclaimers.testnetBody: Everything runs on Sepolia. The PULSO you receive from the faucet and the one you stake have no economic value, and the faucet is intentionally abusable (details in DESIGN.md): it's an educational demo, not a financial product.
disclaimers.paperBody: The trading bots (Bots module) don't execute any real order against any exchange. All PnL, positions and history are simulated on real prices for illustrative purposes only.
disclaimers.aiBody: The assistant (Groq) only responds with the data it sees on screen, never makes up prices or figures, and is forbidden from giving financial advice. None of its answers is an investment recommendation.
disclaimers.footer: Nothing in PULSO —not this page, not the rest of the app, not the AI assistant— constitutes financial, legal or tax advice. Use it to learn and to try out the full flow of a non-custodial exchange, not to make real investment decisions.

education.title: Education
education.subtitle: 8 short lessons to navigate crypto with your own judgment: wallets and seeds, staking, DeFi risks, stablecoins and the dollar in Argentina, how to read charts, smart contracts, custodial vs. non-custodial, and how to spot scams.
